name: Renan Santos Maia
title: Civil Engineer · PhD Candidate (University of Illinois)
tagline: Transportation Engineering - Infrastructure, Pavement Engineering, Asphalt Materials, Geotechnics - Materials to Network-Level Scales
bio_short: Civil Engineer and PhD Candidate at the University of Illinois Urbana-Champaign (#1 U.S. Civil Engineering Program, 2025–2026): Transportation Infrastructure, Pavement Design, Evaluation, Maintenance & Rehabilitation, Geotechnical Engineering, Sustainability and Safety.
bio_full:
I am a PhD Candidate in Civil Engineering at the University of Illinois Urbana-Champaign, where I serve as a Graduate Research Assistant at the Illinois Center for Transportation. My doctoral research bridges flexible pavement material behavior and structural response to inform agency-level decision-making — integrating laboratory characterization, field evaluation, mechanistic modeling, and network-scale management.

My professional trajectory spans over a decade across research, field, and industry environments in Brazil, the United Kingdom, and the United States. I have led applied research programs for IDOT, worked as a Civil Engineer on Brazil's Transnordestina Railway, and held field and plant roles in asphalt construction and airport pavement QA/QC.

I hold a B.Sc. in Civil Engineering from the Federal University of Ceará (with an exchange year at the University of Birmingham, UK), an M.Sc. in Transportation Engineering, and an expected Ph.D. in 2026. My GPA at UIUC is 3.93/4.00.
personal:
Beyond engineering, I am driven by the human side of infrastructure — the communities roads, railways, and pavements serve, and the people who build and maintain them.

I am Brazilian, have lived in the UK, and have spent the last several years in the American Midwest. Moving between cultures has taught me to listen carefully, communicate across difference, and find common ground fast — skills I consider just as important in engineering as any technical method.
